title: Acquiring synchronization and system information in energy saving networks associated with compact minimum system information
abstract: Systems, methods, and instrumentalities are described herein for acquiring synchronization and system information in energy saving networks associated with compact minimum system information. Compact minimum system information may be received. Compact minimum system information may be used to determine system information (SI) and a SI digest associated with the SI. A random access channel (RACH) parameter may be determined based on compact minimum system information (e.g., SI digest).
claims: Claims CLAIMS What Is Claimed Is: 1 . A wireless transmit/receive unit (WTRU) comprising, a processor configured to: receive, from a first cell, system information (SI) associated with a neighbor cell and a first SI digest associated with the SI; perform cell re-selection to the neighbor cell; receive a transmission from the neighbor cell that comprises compact minimum system information associated with the neighbor cell; determine a second SI digest associated with the neighbor cell based on the compact minimum system information; determine whether the first SI digest matches the second SI digest; determine a random access channel (RACH) parameter based on the determination of whether the first SI digest matches the second SI digest; and send a RACH transmission via the neighbor cell, wherein the RACH transmission uses the RACH parameter. 2. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein based on a determination that the first SI digest matches the second SI digest, the RACH parameter is determined based on at least one of the first SI digest or the second SI digest. 3. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the transmission from the neighbor cell that comprises compact minimum system information associated with the neighbor cell is a first transmission, and wherein the processor is further configured to: receive, based on a determination that the first SI digest does not match the second SI digest, a second transmission associated with the neighbor cell, wherein the RACH parameter is determined based on the second transmission associated with the neighbor cell. 4. The WTRU of claim 3, wherein the first transmission from the neighbor cell is a synchronization sequence block (SSB) with the second SI digest. 5. The WTRU of claim 3, wherein the second transmission associated with the neighbor cell is a system information block (SIB), and wherein the second transmission associated with the neighbor cell is received during a non-network energy savings (NES) SSB cycle. 6. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the first SI digest comprises a first value, wherein the second SI digest comprises a second value, and wherein the determination whether the first SI digest matches the second SI digest is further based on the first value and the second value. 7. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the transmission from the neighbor cell that comprises compact minimum system information associated with the neighbor cell is received during a network energy savings (NES) SSB cycle. 8. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the transmission using the RACH via the neighbor cell indicates stored system information. 9. A method, the method comprising, receiving, from a first cell, system information (SI) associated with a neighbor cell and a first SI digest associated with the SI; performing cell re-selection to the neighbor cell; receiving a transmission from the neighbor cell that comprises compact minimum system information associated with the neighbor cell; determining a second SI digest associated with the neighbor cell based on the compact minimum system information; determining whether the first SI digest matches the second SI digest; determining a random access channel (RACH) parameter based on the determination of whether the first SI digest matches the second SI digest; and sending a RACH transmission via the neighbor cell, wherein the RACH transmission uses the RACH parameter. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein based on a determination that the first SI digest matches the second SI digest, the RACH parameter is determined based on at least one of the first SI digest or the second SI digest. 11 . The method of claim 9, wherein the transmission from the neighbor cell that comprises compact minimum system information associated with the neighbor cell is a first transmission, and wherein the method further comprises: receiving, based on a determination that the first SI digest does not match the second SI digest, a second transmission associated with the neighbor cell, wherein the RACH parameter is determined based on the second transmission associated with the neighbor cell. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the first transmission from the neighbor cell is a synchronization sequence block (SSB) with the second SI digest. 13. The method of claim 11 , wherein the second transmission associated with the neighbor cell is a system information block (SIB), and wherein the second transmission associated with the neighbor cell is received during a non-network energy savings (NES) SSB cycle. 14. The method of claim 9, wherein the first SI digest comprises a first value, wherein the second SI digest comprises a second value, and wherein the determination whether the first SI digest matches the second SI digest is further based on the first value and the second value. 15. The method of claim 9, wherein the transmission from the neighbor cell that comprises compact minimum system information associated with the neighbor cell is received during a network energy savings (NES) SSB cycle. 16. The method of claim 9, wherein the transmission using the RACH via the neighbor cell indicates stored system information.
